var: # Site configuration: Baiyun Mountain Scenic Area - Guangzhou
# Sources: Guangzhou Baiyun Mountain Administration Bureau 2022;

detail: Unique identifier used for output folder naming
Official park name (Chinese: Baiyun Shan Fengjing Mingsheng Qu)
Non-native/Invasive Species Forest - significant invasive species dominance
Primary service target: environmental protection and air quality regulation
Annual management budget units (scaled; see budget_annual below)
Number of manager agents (large area divided into management zones)
Year of formal scenic area designation (modern management era)
Documented invasive plant species (Mikania micrantha and Lantana camara dominant)
Standard deviation of monthly mean temperatures (low - warm year-round)
Coefficient of variation of monthly precipitation
GDP per capita (converted from CNY at 7.1 CNY/USD)

Mean years of education (urban adult population)

Documented annual visitor count (one of most visited parks in China)
Fraction of visitors arriving by public transport

value: Baiyun Mountain (2879 ha) is one of the most visited urban forests in China and a designated national scenic area. Invasive species - particularly Mikania micrantha (mile-a-minute weed) and Lantana camara - are severe management challenges owing to the warm humid year-round climate that enables rapid spread. The park serves a critical environmental protection function for Guangzhou as urban green lungs. High visitor pressure combined with severe invasive encroachment creates persistent management conflicts between conservation and access goals.
